commit.message:
fix: Telegram "no response generated" — synthesis, person fields, calendar range (#44)

Three root causes for empty/wrong Telegram responses:

1. When the LLM exhausted all 5 tool rounds, the synthesis round ran
   without tools AND without any instruction to produce text. The LLM
   saw a conversation ending in tool results and produced nothing.
   Fix: add explicit synthesis instruction + fallback from tool results.

2. person_info omitted birthday, company, and position fields — data
   from Apple Contacts was fetched but never shown to the LLM.

3. Calendar search hardcoded ±30 days, missing recurring events like
   birthdays months away. Widened default to ±180 days.

Also: gitignore live e2e test file (contains real names for integration
tests against live server).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct profit factor calculation to standard definition

Was: avgWin / avgLoss (ignores win/loss count ratio)
Now: grossProfit / grossLoss (standard trading metric)

With 25% WR this changes PF from ~3.5 to ~1.2, which correctly
reflects the marginal profitability shown by 0.2% ROI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use JS event dispatch for React-compatible login + anti-bot measures

React apps don't pick up Selenium send_keys values because the native
input setter bypasses React's synthetic event system. Now uses JavaScript
to set input values via the native setter and dispatches input/change
events so React state updates properly.

Also adds:
- Realistic user-agent string for headless Chrome
- CDP-based webdriver property hiding (more robust than execute_script)
- Navigator plugins/languages spoofing
- Enter key fallback if login button not found

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: admin chat, autoadmin system, command chat suppression, announce preview

- .a <text> — admin-only chat (visible only to online admins)
- .admins — list online administrators
- .autoadmin add|remove|list <player|SteamID> — manage auto-login list
- Auto-add to autoadmin on first successful .adminauth
- Auto-grant admin rights on connect if SteamID in autoadmin list
- Suppress .command messages from appearing in public chat
- Replace 📢 emoji with [!] (TMP-compatible) in announcements
- GrantAdminRights helper via ServerBootstrapSystem reflection + fallback

commit.message:
Fix online score/winner sync: finalization window + totalScore sync

- Add 20-frame finalization window when round timer hits 0: fight loop
  keeps running so final score packets exchange before _endRound fires
- Expand RTC packet to 9 bytes: add totalScore (uint16 * 10)
- Sync both roundScore and totalScore continuously so accumulated
  scores across rounds always agree on both clients
- Remote player's totalScore is mirrored each frame, preventing
  compounding discrepancy across multiple rounds

commit.message:
Replace chars/4 estimates with real tiktoken token counts

Tokenized all raw benchmark snapshots using tiktoken cl100k_base instead of
the chars÷4 approximation. Key findings:
- chars/4 underestimates actual tokens by 4-20% depending on format
- Different formats tokenize at different rates (browser-use 3.3 chars/tok,
  cdp 3.6, chrome-devtools 3.2, Playwright 3.8)
- Updated all tables with real token counts
- Methodology now explains tokenization approach and why chars/4 was wrong

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Root cause: .modal-overlay z-index was 950, bottom-nav z-index is 1000,
so the nav bar rendered on top of the modal and covered the submit button.

Also moved padding-bottom back to .add-form (normal spacing only).

expected. Now tries 70B first, falls back to llama-3.1-8b-instant
if rate-limited. 8B is less capable but has much higher limits.

Initial scaffold: transformer with low-rank bottleneck injection

  and two activations (linear, ReLU) for a 2x2 experimental grid

close command blocks, allowing prompt injection. Now XML-escapes
name, status (attr) and output (body) in CommandExecutionResult::block.

* feat: auto-push metadata to Emby/Jellyfin on field save (#409)

When a user updates or clears a metadata field (biography, genres, etc.),
the change is now automatically pushed to every enabled Emby/Jellyfin
connection that has a platform ID mapping for the artist.

Each connection push runs in its own goroutine with a 30-second timeout
derived from context.Background(), so the push never blocks or cancels
with the HTTP response. DB save always succeeds regardless of push outcome.
Errors are logged server-side only. Lidarr and disabled connections are
silently skipped.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: suppress gosec G118 on intentional context.Background() in goroutine

The goroutine must outlive the HTTP request context (which is cancelled when
the response is sent), so context.Background() with an explicit 30s timeout
is the correct choice here. Added nolint comment to explain the intent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: move gosec G118 nolint to the go statement line

golangci-lint reports G118 on the go func() line, not the
context.WithTimeout line. Move the nolint directive accordingly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Fix KeyError in generate_static.py - switch from .format() to str.replace()

CSS curly braces were being interpreted as format placeholders. Switched to
explicit str.replace() calls and removed double-brace escaping from JS.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Persist PTY output to DB for terminal history replay (#6)

routePTYOutput now calls AppendSessionEvent for every PTY frame,
storing raw bytes with a monotonic per-session sequence number.
When the terminal modal reopens, the existing frontend replay
code fetches /api/sessions/:id/events and writes them to xterm.js,
restoring the full scrollback history.

Sequence counters are cleaned up on TypeSessionStopped to avoid
unbounded memory growth.

commit.message:
web: remove duplicate Sentry.init() from sentry.client.config.ts

Both sentry.client.config.ts and instrumentation-client.ts were calling
Sentry.init(), causing "Multiple Sentry Session Replay instances are not
supported" crash on the client.

Keep ONLY instrumentation-client.ts (Next.js 15 convention) which has
Replay, enableLogs, sendDefaultPii, and onRouterTransitionStart.
sentry.client.config.ts is now a no-op comment file — it must exist but
should not call Sentry.init().
